headline: Equinix to create new Malaysia data centre with over $190 million investment
summary: U.S.-listed data centre company Equinix will create a new data ‌centre in Kuala Lumpur with an ‌investment of over $190 million, its fourth such facility in Malaysia, ​the company said in a statement on Tuesday.  A "substantial portion" of ⁠total capacity will support advanced liquid cooling solutions to meet the demands of artificial intelligence and ​high-performance computing, ​it added.  "The development ​is part of Equinix's ‌long-term expansion strategy in Malaysia, with future growth potential supported by adjacent land secured for future expansion, reinforcing Kuala Lumpur's role as a key interconnection hub in ASEAN," it said.
